name: ExtensionNutritionOrder_EnteralFormula_Additive
title: R5: Components to add to the feeding
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` 0..* `BackboneElement`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
*  R5: `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` 0..* `BackboneElement`
*  R4B: `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula` 0..1 `BackboneElement`
*  R4: `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula` 0..1 `BackboneElement`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` allows multiple values in the source, but is mapped to an element that does not allow multiple values in the target.
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.

element.definition: R5: `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` additional types (CodeableReference(http://hl7.org/fhir/StructureDefinition/NutritionProduct)) additional types from child elements (reference)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additiveType` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.
The mappings for `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: reference.
element.requirements:
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` is mapped to FHIR R4 element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additiveType` as `SourceIsBroaderThanTarget`.
The mappings for `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` do not cover the following types: CodeableReference.
The mappings for `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.type` do not cover the following types based on type expansion: reference.

element.short: R5: Reference to a concept (by class) (new)
element.comment:
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is part of an existing definition because parent element `CodeableReference` requires a cross-version extension.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `CodeableReference` is not mapped.
Element `CodeableReference.concept` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: Amount of additive to be given or mixed in (new)
element.comment:
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.quantity` is part of an existing definition because parent element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive` requires an extension for additional cardinality.
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.quantity` has a context of NutritionOrder.enteralFormula based on following the parent source element upwards and mapping to `NutritionOrder`.
Element `NutritionOrder.enteralFormula.additive.quantity` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: Amount of additive to be given or mixed in
